ChangeRequest.name: Possible limitation to applying multiple subtype levels when using the 'role construct', e.g., `Exchange tradable derivative position role`
ChangeRequest.comment:
<!-- See: documentation on [Issues in GitLab](https://gitlab.sofa.dev/sdd-bird-team/bird-logical-data-model/-/wikis/Issues-in-GitLab) -->
# Overview
There exists a logical constraint for applying multiple subtype levels to the 'role construct'. Namely, if a 'thing' can act in multiple 'thing roles' which are organised / subtypes of a 'particular thing role' (being a subype of 'thing role') multiple subtype levels are not valid due to the constraints given by the primary key (of the 'thing role' entity type).
One situation where such a constraint may apply is the entity type `Exchange tradable derivative position role`.
### Contact person
dominik.lin-ully@oenb.at
### Background information
It seems that there is a possible limitations of the BIRD LDM as regards the entity type `Exchange tradable derivative position role`. Specifically the attribute `Exchange tradable derivative position role`.`Exchange tradable derivative position role type` is constraint to the following allowed values: * `Exchange tradable derivative asset or liability position (3)`, and * `Exchange tradable derivative position as a hedge (4)`.
However the `Exchange tradable derivative position role` hierarchy comprises multiple levels, as illustrated in the following picture:
![BIRD_LDM_-_Exchange_tradable_derivative_position_role](/uploads/d516686a7746d93f313d6e7c7494d669/BIRD_LDM_-_Exchange_tradable_derivative_position_role.png)
In my opinion there is a logical problem if an `Exchange tradable derivative position` acts in more than one `Exchange tradable derivative position role` being a subtype of the first level of subtyping, e.g., if an `Exchange tradable derivative position` acts in the roles `Exchange tradable derivative asset position (1)` and `Exchange tradable derivative liability position (2)` (both being subtypes of `Exchange tradable derivative asset or liability position (3)`).
In order to illustrate the (possible) problem more clearly, please consider the following example - for the sake of simplicity we are omitting primary key components originating from the `Model context` entity type and `Party role type`(s):
Two `Parties`, namely `Valentina` and `Dom`, act as the `Buyer` and the `Seller` in two `Exchange tradable derivative position`s involving a the same `Exchange tradable derivative`. One of these positions would be considered a `Exchange tradable derivative asset position`, the other one a `Exchange tradable derivative liability position`.
The primary key of the entity type `Exchange tradable derivative position role` is defined by the following attributes: * Exchange Tradable Derivative Identifier [pk], * Buyer Identifier [pk], * Seller Identifier [pk], * Exchange Tradable Derivative Position Role Type [pk] with allowed values `Exchange tradable derivative asset or liability position` and `Exchange tradable derivative position as a hedge`.
This primary key does not allow to represent the above described situation because both `Exchange tradable derivative position`s would be classified as `Exchange tradable derivative asset or liability position`. The difference between the two objects is the classification as `Exchange tradable derivative asset position`, while the other is a `Exchange tradable derivative liability position` which is captured via the (non-primary key) attribute `Exchange tradable derivative asset or liability position`.`Exchange tradable derivative asset or liability position role type`.
If the given example is not a valid use case, i.e., if the situation where the same `Buyer` and `Seller` are involved in an `Exchange tradable derivative position` with the same `Exchange tradable derivative`, one being an 'asset' (`Exchange tradable derivative asset position`), the other one a 'liability' (`Exchange tradable derivative liability position`), of course there is no issue with this modeling construct.
On the other hand, if a 'thing' can act in multiple 'thing roles' which are organised as subtypes of a particular 'thing role' at the same time, this modeling construct is not valid.
Please also check, make sure that the applied modeling construct is valid where applied.
### Points/Questions to resolve
* validate the claim that there is a constraint related to using multiple subtype levels applied to the 'role construct' * if there exists this limitation, ensure that this situation is not present in the BIRD LDM * if the situation exists, adjust the model accordingly
### Related issues
### Before closing checklist - [ ] Assignee for review - [ ] Assignee to close - [ ] Other actions
